sourceMovie.title: The Shape of Water
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Shape of Water works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Edward Scissorhands
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Shape of Water clicked for you, Edward Scissorhands (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Shape of Water?
faq.answer: Try Edward Scissorhands, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button, Ruby Sparks—each shares DNA with The Shape of Water while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Shape of Water?
faq.answer: The Shape of Water (2017) is best described as Drama, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Shape of Water worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
